activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, principles and processes of Biology
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Describe the physiological, anatomical and ecological processes of living organisms
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explain the basic concepts of Biology (meaning, branches, basic terminologies, importance and its relationship with other disciplines)
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss meaning of biology, basic biological terminologies and branches of biology
Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on importance of biology in our life
Jigsaw: Guide students in manageable groups, to explain the relationship of Biology with other disciplines through jigsaw
activities.assessment_criteria: The basic concept of Biology are clearly explained
suggested_resources: Charts, relevant text, and online resources on basic concept of biology

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, principles and processes of Biology
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Describe the physiological, anatomical and ecological processes of living organisms
activities.learning_activity: (b) Describe a cell (meaning,types, structures and functions)
suggested_methods: Group Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss concept of cell
ICT-Based learning: Guide students to explain cell structures and cell differentiation through simulation
Experimentation: Guide students to prepare microscope slides of a plant and animal cell
activities.assessment_criteria: The concept of cell is described clearly
suggested_resources: Cells charts/ models, light microscope, slide covers, microscope slides, and cells online resources

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, principles and processes of Biology
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Describe the physiological, anatomical and ecological processes of living organisms
activities.learning_activity: (c) Describe nutrition in plants (photosynthesis, leaf structure and mineral requirements)
suggested_methods: Field visit: Guide students to collect different plant leaves around the school compound to observe and explain the external structure of leaves
Group Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss the concept of nutrition in plants, internal structure of a leaf and how photosynthesis occurs in the leaf
Guest speaker: Invite guest speaker (Agricultural extension officer) to share with students on mineral requirements in plants
activities.assessment_criteria: Nutrition in plants is described clearly
suggested_resources: Relevant equipment and apparati, heat sources, water, relevant chemicals and reagents, leaves, pond weeds, nutrition in plants models/ charts, and nutrition in plants online resources

activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of scientific biological terminologies
activities.learning_activity: (a) Explain the concept and systems of classification (meaning ,importance of classification, rules of scientific naming of organisms, artificial and natural classification systems)
suggested_methods: Field visit: Guide students to collect various living things around the school compound and group all the items according to their similarities
Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on the concept of classification
Group Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss systems of classification and scientific rules of naming organisms
Project: Guide students to assign scientific names to organisms and label trees around their school environment
activities.assessment_criteria: Concept and systems of classification explained clearly
suggested_resources: Variety of living organisms, classification charts, and classification online resources

activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of scientific biological terminologies
activities.learning_activity: (b) Describe the major groups of organisms: (Monera, Protista, Fungi, Plantae, Animalia and Viruses)
suggested_methods: Field vist: Guide students in groups to collect various living organisms around the school compound which are representatives of the major groups of organisms and classify them
Group Discussion: Guide students in manageable groups, to identify the major groups of organisms and viruses, discuss their features and economic importance
activities.assessment_criteria: The major groups of organisms and viruses are described clearly
suggested_resources: Variety of living organisms, preserved specimens, charts on major groups of organisms, and classification online resources

activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic skills for conducting biological investigations
activities.learning_activity: (a) Describe the basic skills for conducting biological experiments (observation, measurement and experimentation)
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Guide students to visit biology laboratory to identify common apparatus and equipment used in biological experiments
Experimentation: Guide students to perform activities that involve observation, measurement and experimentation
activities.assessment_criteria: Basic skills for conducting biological experiments are correctly described
suggested_resources: Basic skills for conducting biological expeiments online resources, colourful pictures, perfume, hot water, preserved specimen, live plants and animals specimens, thermometer, weighing scales, rulers, boxes, stones, variety of equipment and apparati used in conducting biological experiments

activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic skills for conducting biological investigations
activities.learning_activity: (b) Carry out microscopic observation of a cell
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to explore procedures that are required to observe a cell under a microscope
Laboratory practical: Guide students to observe cells of living things under light microscope
activities.assessment_criteria: Microscopic observation of a cells carried out correctly
suggested_resources: Light microscope, slide covers, microscope slide, online resources on microscopic observation of a cell and relevant apparati

activities.specific_competence: 3.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic skills for conducting biological investigations
activities.learning_activity: (c) Carry out investigations related to photosynthesis (conditions necessary for photosynthesis)
suggested_methods: Experimentation: Guide students to carry out investigations on the conditions necessary for photosynthesis
activities.assessment_criteria: Investigations related to photosynthesis are carried out correctly
suggested_resources: Variety of equipment and apparati, live plant specimens, relevant chemicals, reagents and photosynthesis online resources

activities.specific_competence: 3.2 Prepare and present results of biological investigations
activities.learning_activity: (a) Prepare and present a report on cell and photosynthesis investigations
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Guide students in groups to discuss the steps in preparation of biological investigation report
Project: Guide students to prepare a report using the steps in preparation of a biological investigation report
Presentation: Guide students to present a biological investigation report
activities.assessment_criteria: Investigation report on cell and photosynthesis is prepared and presented correctly
suggested_resources: Relevant text on steps for scientific investigations and report writing, online resources on report writing

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, principles and processes of Biology
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Describe the physiological, anatomical and ecological processes of living organisms
activities.learning_activity: (a) Describe nutrition in human and ruminants (nutrients, digestive system and processes)
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on the concept of nutrition in human and ruminants
Jigsaw: Guide students to explore nutrients, nutritional deficiency and disorders through jigsaw
ICT-Based learning: Guide student to explain the digestive system, digestion processes in human and ruminants through simulation
Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss diseases and disorders of human digestive system and diseases of human digestive system
activities.assessment_criteria: Nutrition in human and ruminants is described clearly
suggested_resources: Variety of food substances
charts/ pictures displaying variety of food nutrients
models/charts of human and ruminant digestive system
dissected mammal specimen displaying digestive system
charts/ photographs showing common disorders

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, principles and processes of Biology
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Describe the physiological, anatomical and ecological processes of living organisms
activities.learning_activity: (b) Describe the mechanism of transportation of materials in flowering plants (vascular system, absorption and movement of water and mineral salts and transpiration)
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on the concept of transportation of materials in flowering plants
Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss vascular system
ICT based learning: Guide students to explain absorption and movement of water and mineral salts in plants and transpiration process through simulation
Question and answers: Guide students to explain factors affecting rate of transpiration through questions and answers
activities.assessment_criteria: Mechanism of transportation of materials in flowering plants is described clearly
suggested_resources: Charts/model/ photographs showing transportation of materials in plants
relevant equipment and apparati
stain
potted plants
germinated seeds
transportation of materials in plants online resources

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, principles and processes of Biology
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Describe the physiological, anatomical and ecological processes of living organisms
activities.learning_activity: (c) Describe the mechanism of transportation of materials in the human body (the mammalian heart, blood vessels, blood, blood circulation)
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on the concept of transportation of materials in human body
Field trip: Guide students to visit nearby health facility to learn about blood cells, blood groups, blood pressure and blood transfusion
ICT-Based learning: Guide students to explain the structure of the heart and blood circulation in human through simulation
Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss the importance of blood circulation, diseases and disorders of the human circulatory system
activities.assessment_criteria: The mechanism of transportation of materials in the human body are described clearly
suggested_resources: Mammalian heart models/ charts/ photographs
blood circulatory system online resources
dissected mammal displaying the heart and major blood vessels
charts showing diseases and disorders of human circulatory system

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, principles and processes of Biology
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Describe the physiological, anatomical and ecological processes of living organisms
activities.learning_activity: (d) Describe the mechanisms of gaseous exchange and respiration in mammals (gaseous exchange in mammals, aerobic and anaerobic respiration)
suggested_methods: Brainstorm: Guide students to brainstorm the concept of gaseous exchange and respiration in mammals
ICT-based learning: Guide students to explain the mechanism of gaseous exchange and respiration in mammals through simulation/ animation/ video
Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss on factors affecting the rate of gaseous exchange and respiration in mammals
Guest speaker: Invite guest speaker to share with students on various infections and diseases of respiratory system
Exploration: Guide students to explore how aerobic and anaerobic respiration are applied in their real life
activities.assessment_criteria: The mechanisms of gaseous exchange and respiration in mammals are described clearly
suggested_resources: Models/ photographs/ charts showing structure of the lungs and alveolus in human
online resources on gaseous exchange and respiration in mammals

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, principles and processes of Biology
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Describe the physiological, anatomical and ecological processes of living organisms
activities.learning_activity: (e) Describe the mechanism of gaseous exchange in plants (parts of plants responsible for gaseous exchange and the process)
suggested_methods: Questions and answers: Guide students to explain the concept of gaseous exchange in plants through question and answers
ICT based learning: Guide students to explain the mechanism of gaseous exchange in plants through simulation / animation/video
activities.assessment_criteria: The mechanism of gaseous exchange in plants is described clearly
suggested_resources: Plant leaves
photographs showing the structure of stomata
potted plants
charts/pictures of gaseous exchange in plants
gaseous exchange in plants online resources

activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic skills for conducting biological investigations
activities.learning_activity: (a) Investigate food nutrients in various food samples
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to explore the scientific procedures required to conduct food test
Laboratory practical: Guide students to investigate food nutrients in various food samples
activities.assessment_criteria: Food nutrients in various food samples are investigated correctly using the scientific procedures
suggested_resources: Relevant reagents and chemicals
various food samples
apparati and source of heat

activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic skills for conducting biological investigations
activities.learning_activity: (b) Investigate processes of transportation of materials in flowering plants
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss procedures/ steps for investigating processes of transportation of materials in flowering plants
Experimentation: Guide students to investigate processes of transportation of materials in flowering plants
activities.assessment_criteria: Processes of transportation of materials in flowering plants are investigated correctly
suggested_resources: Diagram/ video showing transverse section of living plant tissues
mounted slides of plant tissues
relevant reagents and chemicals
source of heat
plants with variegated leaves
distilled water
ringed plant
charts/ video showing transportation of materials in plants
online resources on transportation of materials in plants

activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic skills for conducting biological investigations
activities.learning_activity: (c) Carry out experiments to determine the pulse rate in human
suggested_methods: Experimentation: Guide students to carry out experiment to determine pulse rate among themselves
activities.assessment_criteria: Experiments to determine the pulse rate in human are carried out correctly
suggested_resources: Blood pressure machine
smartphone app
digital fitness trackers and exercise machine

activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic skills for conducting biological investigations
activities.learning_activity: (d) Investigate aerobic and anaerobic respiration in living organisms
suggested_methods: Experimentation: Guide students to investigate aerobic and anaerobic respiration in living organisms through experiment
activities.assessment_criteria: Aerobic and anaerobic respiration in living organisms are investigated correctly
suggested_resources: Charts/ diagrams on aerobic and anaerobic respiration of living organisms

activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Prepare and present results of biological investigations
activities.learning_activity: (a) Prepare and present a report of a biological investigation on ( food nutrients in various food samples, transportation of materials in flowering plants, pulse rate in human, aerobic and anaerobic respiration )
suggested_methods: Project: Guide students to prepare biological investigation report using scientific steps/ procedures
Presentation: Guide students to present a biological investigation report
activities.assessment_criteria: A biological investigation report is prepared and presented correctly using scientific procedures
suggested_resources: Steps / procedures for biological investigations online resources

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, principles and processes of Biology
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Describe the physiological, anatomical and ecological processes of living organisms
activities.learning_activity: (a) Describe coordination in plants (tropic, responses) and animals (nervous coordination in human, structure of neurones, sense organs and endocrine system)
suggested_methods: Brainstorm: Guide students to brainstorm on the concept of coordination in plants and animals
Jigsaw: Guide students to discuss types of tropic responses in plants through jigsaw
Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss nervous coordination in human, structure of neurons, sense organs and endocrine system in human
activities.assessment_criteria: Coordination in plants and animals described clearly
suggested_resources: Models/pictures of sense organs, young plants, charts showing plants tropic responses, models/charts/ pictures showing structure of neurones and online resouces on plants and animals coordination

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, principles and processes of Biology
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Describe the physiological, anatomical and ecological processes of living organisms
activities.learning_activity: (b) Describe the mechanism of excretion in the human body (systems: organs, products)
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on the concept of excretion in human
Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss excretory organs, products and urinary system
ICT based learning: Guide students to explain common complications and disorders of human excretory system through simulation/ video
activities.assessment_criteria: The mechanism of excretion in human body is described clearly
suggested_resources: Models/ charts/ pictures of human urinary system, picture of mammalian kidney, online resources on human excretion and charts illustrating disoders of human excretory system

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, principles and processes of Biology
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Describe the physiological, anatomical and ecological processes of living organisms
activities.learning_activity: (c) Describe the mechanism of regulation in the human body (temperature regulation, blood sugar regulation and osmoregulation)
suggested_methods: Question and answers: Guide students to explain the concept of regulation in human body through questions and answers
Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss mechanism of temperature regulation, blood sugar regulation and osmoregulation
ICT based learning: Guide students to demonstrate mechanisms of temperature regulation by using digital simulation or videos
activities.assessment_criteria: The mechanism of regulation in the human body is described clearly
suggested_resources: Charts on temperature and blood sugar regulation and online resouces of temperature regulation, blood sugar regulation and osmoregulation

activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic skills for conducting biological investigations
activities.learning_activity: (a) Investigate the process of nervous coordination (reflex arc and knee jerk)
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss the processes involved during reflex arc and knee jerk
ICT based learning: Guide students to demonstrate the process of nervous coordination by using a digital simulations or videos
Experimentation: Guide students to demonstrate a simple experiment on the human nervous coordination that involves knee jerk
activities.assessment_criteria: The process of nervous coordination is investigated correctly
suggested_resources: Sharp objects, hot objects, animal toys models (snake, scorpion), live small animals specimens and nervous coordination online resources

activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic skills for conducting biological investigations
activities.learning_activity: (b) Investigate tropic responses in plants
suggested_methods: Project : Organize students in groups to perform experiment to investigate tropic responses in plants
activities.assessment_criteria: Tropic responses in plants are investigated correctly
suggested_resources: Variety of plants Seedlings, pots, seeds, soil, water, boxes and online resources/ charts on tropic responses in plants

activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Carry out a biological project work using biological principles
activities.learning_activity: (a) Develop and carry out a simple research project in Biology
suggested_methods: Group discussion: Guide students to discuss the principles of carrying out a biological research project
Experiment: Guide students to carry out a biological research project using the required principles
activities.assessment_criteria: A biological research project work is developed and carried out correctly
suggested_resources: Text/online resources on principles of biological projects

activities.main_competence: 1.0 Demonstrate mastery of the concepts, principles and processes of Biology
activities.specific_competence: 1.1 Describe the physiological, anatomical and ecological processes of living organisms
activities.learning_activity: (a) Describe reproduction in plants (concept of reproduction in plants, asexual and sexual reproduction, pollination and fertilization)
suggested_methods: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on the concept of reproduction in plants
Group discussion:Guide students in manageable groups to discuss on the asexual and sexual reproduction, pollination and fertilization in plants
activities.assessment_criteria: Reproduction in plants is described clearly
suggested_resources: Reproduction in plants online resources, potted plants, hibiscus flowers, bean flowers, elephant grass flowers, hand lens, surgical/ razor blade, pictures/ illustration showing reproduction in plants

activities.specific_competence: (b) Describe reproduction in humans (sexual reproduction in human, fertilization, pregnancy and child birth)
activities.learning_activity: Question and answers: Guide students to explain the concept of reproduction in humans through question and answers: ICT based learning: Guide students to explore mechanism of meiosis through digital simulation Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss sexual reproduction, fertilization, pregnancy and child birth in human
suggested_methods: Question and answers
ICT based learning
Group discussion
activities.assessment_criteria: Reproduction in humans is described clearly
suggested_resources: Charts/ models of human reproductive system
charts/models/pictures of meiosis stages
online resources of reproduction in human
charts of family planning methods

activities.specific_competence: (c) Describe the mechanisms of growth in mammals and flowering plants (concept of growth, mitosis and growth, growth and developmental stages in human and seed germination)
activities.learning_activity: Guest speaker: Invite guest speaker to share with students on family planning and contraception and disorders of reproductive system Brainstorm: Guide students to brainstorm on the concept of growth in flowering plants and mammals ICT based learning: Guide students to explain stages of mitosis, growth and developmental stages in human through digital simulation
suggested_methods: Guest speaker
Brainstorm
ICT based learning
activities.assessment_criteria: The mechanisms of growth in flowering plants and mammals are described clearly
suggested_resources: Photographs/ charts on growth in flowering plants
growth in plants online resources

activities.specific_competence: (c) Describe the mechanisms of growth in mammals and flowering plants (concept of growth, mitosis and growth, growth and developmental stages in human and seed germination)
activities.learning_activity: Group discussion: Guide students in manageable group to discuss growth process in plants and factors affecting growth in plants and animals Project : Guide students in manageable groups to carry out an experiments on growth in plants and small mammals
suggested_methods: Group discussion
Project
activities.assessment_criteria: The mechanisms of growth in flowering plants and mammals are described clearly

activities.specific_competence: (d) Explore the basic tenets of genetics (genetic materials, principles of inhertance, monohybrid, Mendelian and non-Mendelian inheritance; variation
activities.learning_activity: Brainstorming: Guide students to brainstorm on the concept of genetics Group discussion: Guide students in manageable groups to discuss genetic materials, principles of inheritance and variation ICT based learning; Guide students to demonstrate Mendelian laws of inheritance and non-Mendelian inheritance through simulation Problem solving: Guide students to apply principles of genetics to solve genetic problems
suggested_methods: Brainstorming
Group discussion
ICT based learning
Problem solving
activities.assessment_criteria: The basic tenets of genetics are explored clearly
suggested_resources: Charts/models photographs on basic tenets of genetics
online resources on basic tenets of genetics

activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic skills for conducting biological investigations (a) Carry out investigation related to reproduction in flowering plants (flower structure and embryo)
activities.learning_activity: Practical work: Guide students to make longitudinal and cross-sections of flower to identify reproductive structures and the embryo ICT based learning: Guide students to investigate the reproductive structure of flowering plants through digital simulation
suggested_methods: Practical work
ICT based learning
activities.assessment_criteria: Investigation related to reproduction in flowering plants performed correctly
suggested_resources: Hibiscus flowers
razor blade
forceps
reproduction in plants online resources

activities.specific_competence: 2.1 Demonstrate mastery of basic skills for conducting biological investigations (b) Investigate growth in flowering plants (hypogeal and epigeal germination)
activities.learning_activity: Experimentation: Guide students in manageable groups to set up a simple experiment on the investigation of epigeal and hypogeal germination
suggested_methods: Experimentation
activities.assessment_criteria: Growth in flowering plants is investigated correctly
suggested_resources: Seeds
pots
water
soil
charts/pictures on growth in plants

activities.specific_competence: 2.2 Carry out a biological project work using biological principles (a) Complete and submit a report for the research project started in Form Three
activities.learning_activity: Project activity: Guide students to prepare and submit report of a research project
suggested_methods: Project activity
activities.assessment_criteria: Research project report are completed and timely submitted
suggested_resources: Relevant text on biology research project
online resources on research projects
